news.24h_summary: CRITICAL GEOPOLITICAL AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENTS: The Iran war has reached a critical inflection point with Trump demanding 'unconditional surrender' and the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed (94% reduction in shipping). Oil posted its largest weekly gain in futures history (+35%), while simultaneously weak February jobs data (-92K vs +50K expected, unemployment to 4.4%) creates a stagflationary setup. Fed officials are split between inflation concerns from the oil shock and dovish pivots from labor market weakness. US equity funds saw largest outflows in 8 weeks as investors flee to safety. The combination of supply-side inflation shock and demand-side economic weakness presents the classic stagflation dilemma for markets and Fed policy.

bond.brief: Critical data gap prevents proper analysis. Previous BEARISH_DURATION call based on overheating economy. VIX spike suggests potential regime change. Recommend data sourcing review before positioning.

currency.brief: Flip to USD bullish on risk-off surge (VIX 29.49). Previous bearish call partially worked but risk regime changed. Long USD/CHF, short AUD/USD. Avoid USD/JPY longs given intervention risk at 157.86.

commodities.brief: Strong bullish pivot from previous neutral stance. WTI surged from $76 to $91 with curve flipping to backwardation. Recommend increasing energy allocation targeting $95 WTI. High conviction trade.

metals.brief: Maintain bearish metals stance. Gold $5178 and copper $5.84 remain extreme despite VIX spike. Previous real rate headwinds likely persist. Recommend continued shorts via DUST/JDST.

druckenmiller.headline: I don't see a fat pitch — VIX at 29 says markets are nervous but I need more data to bet the ranch.
druckenmiller.brief: VIX at 29 says something's breaking but I can't see what without Fed data. Raise cash, small commodity hedge, wait for the fat pitch. Don't chase.

news.24h_summary: MARKET IN CRISIS MODE: The Iran war has reached maximum escalation with Trump demanding 'unconditional surrender' and oil posting the largest weekly gain in futures history (+35%). The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed with only 8 ships/day vs normal 120+, creating a supply shock. Meanwhile, February jobs crashed -92k vs +50k expected, unemployment rising to 4.4%, creating Fed policy confusion. The dual shock of energy crisis + economic weakness creates stagflation risk. Oil hitting $90+ validates our BE energy infrastructure thesis massively, but weak jobs data undermines our TLT short position as Fed governors signal dovish pivot. Defense stocks surging on Trump's 4x weapons production announcement. Market regime shifting to extreme risk-off with equity outflows accelerating.

bond.brief: CRITICAL: Complete data outage prevents analysis. VIX spike to 29.49 signals stress. Recommend CASH position and immediate data sourcing review before any duration exposure.

currency.brief: Maintain USD bullish stance. Risk-off surge validates previous flip. Long USD/CHF, short AUD/USD. Avoid USD/JPY longs above 157.80 intervention risk. Target DXY 99.50 breakout.

commodities.brief: Maintain LONG energy stance. WTI consolidating at $91 vs $95 target. Elevated VIX supports risk premiums. Reduce natural gas exposure given contango structure.

metals.brief: Maintain bearish metals stance. Gold $5175 copper $5.84 extreme despite VIX spike. Previous real rate headwinds likely persist. Recommend shorts via DUST/JDST, target gold sub-$4800.

druckenmiller.headline: I don't see a fat pitch — limited data with elevated VIX suggests caution until liquidity picture clarifies.
druckenmiller.brief: Missing Fed data, employment, inflation — can't see the picture. VIX at 29 says something's wrong. Raise cash, wait for clarity. No fat pitches when you're trading blind.
